abstract.text: A 2D vector using integer coordinates.

inlineContent.text: A 2-element structure that can be used to represent 2D grid coordinates or any other pair of integers.
It uses integer coordinates and is therefore preferable to
when exact precision is required. Note that the values are limited to 32 bits, and unlike
this cannot be configured with an engine build option. Use integer or
In a boolean context, a Vector2i will evaluate to
. Otherwise, a Vector2i will always evaluate to

abstract.text: Types that conform to VariantStorable can be stored in a Variant and can be extracted
back out of a Variant.
Up unit vector. Y is down in 2D, so this vector points -Y.
Compares two
vectors by first checking if the X value of the left vector is less than or equal to the X value of the
vector. If the X values are exactly equal, then it repeats this check with the Y values of the two vectors. This operator is useful for sorting vectors.

with no conversion.  These include all of the Variant types from Godot (for

SwiftGodot.Object) as well as the built-in Swift types UInt8, Int64 and Double.

Returns the negative value of the Vector2i. This is the same as writing Vector2i(-v.x, -v.y). This operation flips the direction of the vector while keeping the same magnitude.
